validation_results.name: terraform validate
validation_results.command: cd infra/terraform && terraform init -backend=false -input=false && terraform validate

gate_results.log_path: grep -n 'events-worker|ingestion-worker|notifications-worker' infra/terraform/environments/production/terraform.tfvars -> all 3 present as railway_services map entries (lines 84,93,102) each with an image, replicas, memory, cpu. infra/terraform/main.tf:110 passes var.railway_services into the railway module; modules/railway/main.tf:35 does 'for_each = var.services' over that map — confirmed real for_each wiring, not a hardcoded stub.

definition_of_done_items.criterion: Workers present in variables.tf railway_services + covered by the module for_each; a reproducible Railway deploy path exists for each
definition_of_done_items.evidence: Note on literal wording: the 3 workers are declared in the per-environment tfvars (environments/{dev,staging,production}/terraform.tfvars), not hardcoded into variables.tf's own default map — variables.tf declares the railway_services type/schema and the module for_each consumes whatever map the active environment's tfvars supplies. This is the correct pattern for per-environment service sets and is functionally equivalent to the DoD's intent. All 3 workers (events-worker, ingestion-worker, notifications-worker) verified present in all 3 environment tfvars files.

notes: RETROACTIVE ATTESTATION BACKFILL (ENG-OPS-002.R16, remediating GOV-A-001). No canonical attestation.json existed for this task in Sprint 18 (only task-tracking.json). Primary delivery evidence: PR #289 'feat(infra): events/ingestion/notifications workers + strictly-free cost guards (INFRA-TF-002)' (merged 2026-06-06, commit 5289aa9a43a08305d1d42f1dd43c537386781115) — task ID is literally in the PR title, independently confirmed MERGED via `gh pr view 289`. This closes the GH issues referenced in the task description (#230/#259/#270 — workers built/tested but never deployed) by giving each worker a first-class railway_services entry. Re-verified 2026-07-22 by grepping the current tfvars for all 3 worker names (present in all 3 environments) and confirming the module's for_each actually consumes var.railway_services (not a dead/unused variable). Did not independently re-run `terraform plan` against live Railway (would require live credentials/state access out of scope for an attestation-only backfill task); relied on `terraform validate` (structural correctness) plus static for_each wiring confirmation.
